title: Create a Library
text: Create a Library To create IncludeScripts, Global ProcScripts, and snippets for use during modeling and construction, you use the Project Editor. Before you begin: You must have created a project to contain your application definitions. For more information, see Working with Projects . For other types of objects, see Create a Library for Modeled Properties or Global Objects . To create IncludeScripts, Global ProcScripts, and snippets for use during modeling and construction: Open your project. In the U-Bar, click Browse , select prj , then choose the project you want to open. In the Templates tab of the Resource Browser, select the desired library template. Drag and drop it onto the project in the Structure Editor. Rename the library. Optionally, edit the Description property to provide a more informative description. To open the library, right-click it and choose Open from the menu. In the library editor, drag templates from the Resource Browser and drop them onto the library object in the Structure to create them. Then edit them as required, depending on the object. Related Links Working with Projects Use the Project Editor to create new Main Development Objects, add existing objects to the Project, and compile all objects referenced by the Project, and perform other project-level actions. Libraries Libraries are collections of objects that can be shared in some way, either during development, or at runtime. They are main development objects, so whenever they are saved or exported, all the objects that they contain are included. Naming Rules for Uniface Development Objects The name of a development object is its unique identifier. When naming development objects, use the allowed syntax for the object name. Avoid using words that are reserved for use within Uniface and your own environment, including the operating system and database. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
